answers.field_label: Brief Description Of Your Business And Or Products
answers.value:
Sweetwater Orchard & Creamery is a family-run, pick-your-own fruit farm and small-batch dairy creamery located just outside downtown Lexington, South Carolina. We specialize in seasonal u-pick experiences, including strawberries (April–May), blueberries and blackberries (June–July), and a smaller mixed orchard of peaches and figs (mid-summer). In addition to fresh fruit, we operate a Grade A micro-dairy with a small herd of Jersey and Holstein cows, producing cream-line milk, chocolate milk, buttermilk, and a rotating selection of homemade ice creams and sorbets made with fruit grown right here on the farm.

Our farm store, open March through December, features our own products along with goods from neighboring farms: free-range eggs, local honey, stone-ground grits, jams and jellies, and a limited selection of grass-fed beef and heritage pork from nearby producers. We also offer homemade baked goods on weekends—items like strawberry bread, blackberry cobbler, ice cream cookie sandwiches, and seasonal pies that change based on what is ripe in the fields.

Visitors can enjoy a low-key, family-friendly farm experience. We have a small petting area with goats and chickens, a shaded picnic area overlooking the berry fields, a simple nature trail around our pond, and a play area with a sandbox, tractor tires, and a photo backdrop made from vintage farm equipment. During peak seasons, we provide field wagons and picking buckets, hand-washing stations, and clearly posted pricing so families can comfortably pick as much or as little as they like.

We host school field trips, homeschool days, and small group tours by reservation, focusing on hands-on learning about how fruits are grown, basic soil health, pollinators (we keep several beehives on-site), and how milk goes from the cow to the bottle. For groups, we can include a short hayride, a visit to the milking parlor (viewing from a safe distance), and a small ice cream cup or chocolate milk for each child. We tailor the content to the age group, from preschoolers up through high school agriculture classes.

Seasonally, we offer a few special events such as a spring Strawberry Fest weekend with live local music and food trucks, a summer Blueberry Pancake Morning where we serve house-made pancakes topped with our berries and syrup, and a simple fall Harvest Weekend with pumpkins, a small corn maze suitable for younger kids, and farm photo spots. We do not operate as a haunted attraction; our focus is on daytime, family-oriented activities.

We are committed to sustainable, transparent farming practices. While we are not certified organic, we use integrated pest management (IPM), minimize chemical inputs, focus on soil health through cover crops and composting, and work closely with Clemson Extension for best practices. Our cows are pasture-raised, supplemented with high-quality feed as needed, and we do not use artificial growth hormones. We are happy to answer detailed questions about how we grow and care for our land and animals—our approach is to be as open and educational as possible.

Sweetwater Orchard & Creamery is an ideal outing for families with children, church groups, and anyone looking for fresh, local food and a relaxed farm atmosphere. We recommend sturdy shoes, sun protection, and calling or checking our website and social media for current hours, crop availability, and weather-related updates before your visit. Our goal is for people to leave with delicious fruit and dairy products, a better understanding of where their food comes from, and a few fun, slightly sticky, strawberry-stained memories.
